title: anguina thwaitesii
intro: Anguina thwaitesii, also known as Ceylonese dodder, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae and is native to Sri Lanka. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating cough, and treating various digestive issues. Anguina thwaitesii is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste for its herbal remedies, although specific preparation methods and dosages may vary depending on the intended use and cultural context.
